varieties.name: IRANIAN / PERSIAN SAFFRON 🏆 🇮🇷
varieties.origin: Iran (Persia) - Khorasan, Kerman, Isfahan provinces. Cultivated for 3,000+ years.

varieties.name: Super Negin
varieties.growing_notes: Hand-selected individual stigmas, carefully sorted to select only longest perfect threads
varieties.availability: Premium international suppliers, specialized Persian spice merchants

varieties.name: Sargol
varieties.growing_notes: Style completely removed, includes shorter and broken pieces from sorting process
varieties.availability: Very common, best value for pure quality

varieties.name: Pushal (Pushali)
varieties.growing_notes: Partially separated during processing, traditional presentation style
varieties.availability: Common, good for cooking where volume matters more than premium appearance

varieties.name: Bunch (Dasteh)
varieties.growing_notes: Byproduct of producing premium grades - the leftover style after stigma removal

varieties.name: KASHMIRI SAFFRON 🏆 🌏 🇮🇳
varieties.origin: Kashmir Valley, Jammu & Kashmir, India - Pampore (Saffron Town), Pulwama, Budgam districts

varieties.name: Mongra (Mogra) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Style and stigma completely separated during harvest, stigmas cut precisely at junction point, sun-dried to intensify properties, hand-selected for premium quality, zero floral waste
varieties.availability: Limited - Kashmir production only 7-15 tonnes annually, GI-certified suppliers (Pampore cooperatives), premium Indian spice merchants, export restricted

varieties.name: Lacha (Lachha) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Stigma and style partially separated - 1-2cm style remains attached, sun-dried traditionally, includes broken threads from processing
varieties.availability: More common than Mongra, Kashmiri markets and Indian grocery stores

varieties.name: SPANISH SAFFRON 🏆 🇪🇸
varieties.origin: Spain - La Mancha region (Castilla-La Mancha), Toledo, Albacete, Cuenca

varieties.name: Coupé
varieties.growing_notes: Style completely removed, hand-selected premium threads
varieties.availability: Limited, premium Spanish suppliers, specialty spice importers

varieties.name: La Mancha PDO
varieties.growing_notes: Toasted drying rather than sun-dried, creates distinctive smoky aroma
varieties.availability: Premium Spanish food importers, specialty gourmet shops, must display PDO certification

varieties.name: Rio
varieties.availability: Common in Spanish grocery stores and international markets

varieties.name: Krokos Kozanis PDO
varieties.growing_notes: Style portion commonly retained, sun-dried traditionally, small-scale artisanal production
varieties.availability: Limited international availability, Greek specialty importers, must show PDO certification

varieties.name: ITALIAN SAFFRON 🏆 🇮🇹
varieties.origin: Italy - Multiple regions (Sardinia, Abruzzo, Tuscany, Lombardy, Umbria)

varieties.name: Umbrian Saffron
varieties.availability: Extremely limited, specialty Italian importers, high-end gourmet shops

varieties.name: MOROCCAN SAFFRON 🏆 🇲🇦
varieties.availability: Limited international availability, Moroccan specialty shops, quality highly variable

varieties.name: AFGHAN SAFFRON 🏆 🇦🇫
varieties.availability: Increasing international availability, marketed as premium alternative to Iranian
